channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Rhythm and timing in laughter reveal that human vocal plasticity falls on a hominid continuum | Communications Biology - Nature
item.description:

item.title: In 2004, a magnetar on the far side of the Milky Way unleashed a giant flare so intense it disturbed Earth’s ionosphere from tens of thousands of light-years away — and in just 0.2 seconds released as much energy as the Sun emits in roughly 250,000 years. - Space Daily
item.description: In 2004, a magnetar on the far side of the Milky Way unleashed a giant flare so intense it disturbed Earth’s ionosphere from tens of thousands of light-years away — and in just 0.2 seconds released as much energy as the Sun emits in roughly 250,000 years.  Space Daily

  4. Why Human Body Size Leaped 2 Million Years Ago  Neuroscience News
  5. Human Ancestors Suddenly Got Bigger Around 2 Million Years Ago — With Average Body Size Jumping From 88 to 132 Pounds  Discover Magazine

item.title: The Soviet Union launched nearly 30 spacecraft at Venus and became the only nation ever to land on its surface and send back pictures — color photographs of a scorched volcanic plain under a yellow-green sky, shot in the few dozen minutes before the heat - 19FortyFive

item.title: In 1995, astronomers aimed the Hubble Space Telescope at a tiny patch of sky that looked almost empty — a dark spot near the Big Dipper no wider than a pinhead held at arm’s length. After 10 days of exposures, the darkness resolved into roughly 3,000 gala - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In 1995, astronomers aimed the Hubble Space Telescope at a tiny patch of sky that looked almost empty — a dark spot near the Big Dipper no wider than a pinhead held at arm’s length. After 10 days of exposures, the darkness resolved into roughly 3,000 gala  Space Daily
  2. Hubble Glimpses Merging Galaxy Clusters  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. The History and Scientific Impact of Hubble’s Deep Field Imaging  Universe Today
  4. Hubble Sees Swarm Of Galaxies 18 June  Mirage News
  5. Hubble Captures A Swarm of Galaxies and A Cosmic Telescope  DIYPhotography

item.title: If you could drive a car straight up into the sky at highway speed, you would reach the edge of outer space in under an hour — the only thing protecting every living creature on Earth from the lethal vacuum is a layer of air thinner, relative to the planet, than the s - Space Daily
item.description: If you could drive a car straight up into the sky at highway speed, you would reach the edge of outer space in under an hour — the only thing protecting every living creature on Earth from the lethal vacuum is a layer of air thinner, relative to the planet, than the s  Space Daily
